properties.address: New Pier Street, Walton on the Naze, Essex CO14 8EB
properties.description: Our Warm Hub initiative commenced on 1 st December 2022 and has taken place almost every Thursday since. The Hub was started in response to the cost of living crisis with people experiencing the effects of increasing energy and household bills. In addition, some people, particularly the elderly, were experiencing social isolation and loneliness following the Covid pandemic. Over the winter months the Hub has offered a warm place for people to meet providing refreshments and homemade soup with a roll at lunchtime, all free of charge. We have continued to provide a space to meet and sandwich/refreshments throughout the summer months too. In addition we provide a free gentle exercise class twice a month between 2.00 and 3.00 p.m. which is funded by Active Essex (dates advertised on the website).
properties.title: Emmanuel Church

properties.address: Stutton Community Shop, Ipswich, Suffolk IP9 2TA
properties.description: The space is a community shop and small cafe, entirely run by volunteers. We are open from 9 to 4 during the week and 9 to 11 at weekends, and offer home made cakes and sandwiches as well as tea and coffee.
properties.title: Stutton Community Shop

properties.description: Join us every Tuesday and Thursday from 12:00pm to 2:00pm throughout the summer holidays for our free, food-themed drop-in sessions! Each week has a different theme, with the chance to make and enjoy something tasty, including: Week 1: Pop-in Pizza Week 2: Scoops of Fun (Ice Cream) Week 3: Stay Toastie Week 4: Perfect Pancakes Week 5: Mix It Up (Smoothies) Week 6: Top Dogs (Hot Dogs) Alongside our food activities, there are plenty of games, colouring, and opportunities to meet up with friends, make new ones, and enjoy a relaxed, welcoming space. No booking is required. Spaces are limited to 10 young people at a time and are available on a first come, first served basis. If we're full, we'll do our best to reserve you a space at the next drop-in session. Young people aged 11-13 will need parental or carer consent before attending. This can be given over the phone or in person. Please let a member of staff know about any allergies when you arrive. Our team will always ask before taking part in any food activity.
properties.title: Summer Holiday Drop Ins (Ages 11-18)

properties.address: Landseer Road Methodist Church, Suffolk IP3 9LX
properties.description: Free breakfast for everyone in a warm and welcoming environment.
properties.title: Tea and Toast

properties.description: Teentalk Harwich. Our 11-16 Social Group is a fun, welcoming, and inclusive space where young people can relax, make friends, and try something new. Open to young people all across Tendring to attend Each week we enjoy a variety of activities including arts and crafts, games, cooking and baking, and seasonal projects. It's a great opportunity to build confidence, meet new people, and have fun in a safe and supportive environment. The group runs every Thursday from 4:30pm to 6:30pm. Spaces are limited to 10 young people, so booking is essential. Please contact us to sign up or find out more.
properties.title: Teentalk Harwich 11-16 Social Group

properties.description: Our 17-25 Social Group is a friendly, welcoming, and inclusive space for young people from across Tendring to meet, socialise, and have fun. Each week we enjoy a range of activities including arts and crafts, games, cooking and baking, quizzes, and seasonal projects. It's a great opportunity to make new friends, build confidence, and connect with others in a relaxed and supportive environment. The group runs every Wednesday from 1:00pm to 3:00pm. The group is open to young people aged 17 to 25 from across Tendring. Booking is essential, so please contact us to sign up or find out more.
properties.title: Teentalk Harwich 17-25 Social Group

properties.description: The Link Up is a friendly, welcoming, and inclusive social group for young people aged 11 to 16 who are home educated, experiencing Emotionally Based School Avoidance (EBSA), attending school on a part-time timetable, or not currently in education. The group provides a safe space to connect with others, build friendships, and take part in a range of fun activities, including arts and crafts, games, cooking and baking, and seasonal projects. It offers young people the opportunity to socialise, develop confidence, and enjoy being part of a supportive community. The group runs every Friday from 1:00pm to 3:00pm. Booking is essential, so please contact us to sign up or find out more.
properties.title: Teentalk Harwich The Link Up Social Group

properties.description: COFFEE & CONVERSATIONS - Enjoy a relaxed atmosphere where every one is welcome. Everything is free but donations are gratefully received. Our friends from ‘Right At Home’ will be joining us and often we have crafts available for people to try their hand at something new. CUPPA & CHAT - Come and join us for a friendly social morning with tea, coffee and cake. There’ll be a warm welcome to greet you and new friends to make! For more info on both events email: vicar.allhallows@gmail.com
